Sensing, mapping, and physical computing

Technical projects with real systems behind them.

This site focuses on geospatial workflows, environmental sensing, Arduino-based logging, Python processing, interactive installation systems, and public documentation.

It is designed as a working portfolio: a place to move directly between featured projects, repository structure, scripts, technical notes, and selected hybrid work.

Open OMEGA Browse Projects Public Documentation
Current focus

OMEGA: Oceanic Measurement & Environmental Geospatial Array

A compact underwater survey system that records depth, position, and environmental data from a moving ROV and turns those measurements into bathymetric maps and georeferenced outputs.

Capture GPS, sonar depth, temperature, IMU orientation, SD logging, field filtering
Processing Python scripts for mapping, interpolation, contour generation, and Earth overlays
Output Survey-quality datasets, depth maps, contour maps, KML files, and overlays
Featured project

OMEGA brings sensing, filtering, and mapping into one working system.

The project combines an Arduino-based capture device with a Python-based processing workflow. During acquisition, measurements are filtered using GPS quality, fix age, platform orientation, speed, and spacing constraints; afterward, the resulting mapping dataset is converted into depth maps, interpolated surfaces, contour maps, and georeferenced outputs.

Featured system

Oceanic Measurement & Environmental Geospatial Array

OMEGA is structured to separate capture, validation, and reconstruction into distinct stages. That architecture makes the project useful both as a field tool and as a clear technical case study.

The public repository includes the Arduino logger, Python mapping scripts, documentation, example data, and a visual pipeline diagram for the full workflow.

Capture Arduino logger with GPS, sonar, temperature, IMU, LCD, and SD
Processing CSV parsing, filtering, IDW interpolation, contour generation
Outputs Depth maps, continuous surfaces, KML points, and Earth overlays
GitHub Repository Arduino Logger Python Scripts

System at a glance

  • Quick start commands Depth maps, interpolated maps, contour maps, KML output, and Google Earth overlays are all exposed directly in the repo workflow.
  • Repository structure Code, docs, scripts, and example data are separated cleanly so the project is readable as both software and documentation.
  • Method Filtering is handled during acquisition, then surface reconstruction is handled during processing.
  • Usefulness The project is easy to enter at multiple levels: concept, hardware, code, outputs, or data.

Direct access

Repository entry points that are actually useful.

Instead of a generic index, this front page should take visitors straight into the parts of the project that matter: overview, hardware logger, processing scripts, example data, and technical documentation.

Overview

README

Project summary, quick start commands, architecture, data flow, hardware, outputs, and method.

Arduino

The logging sketch and hardware-side structure for field acquisition and filtered mapping output.

Scripts

Python tools for scatter maps, interpolation, contours, KML export, and Google Earth overlays.

 Technical notes

Docs

Project documentation for the system and the scripts, organized separately from the README.

 Testing

Example Data

Sample mapping files for checking the workflow and generating outputs without new field capture.

 Versions

Tags

Tagged states of the repository for stable reference points and release-oriented browsing.

Use the site

A cleaner front door for the technical work.

The aim here is simple: make the site visually stronger, easier to navigate, and more useful on first visit.

This version is built to function as a technical landing page rather than a placeholder index. The most important links are surfaced early, OMEGA has a true featured position, and the rest of the page supports that project with direct entry points into related work and documentation.

  • Featured first OMEGA is presented as a flagship system rather than a generic card in a grid.
  • Multiple entry points Visitors can enter through the repository, scripts, hardware, docs, or external project pages.
  • More visual contrast A stronger editorial layout, larger typography, and clearer hierarchy make the site feel designed.
  • OMEGA Repository Primary public project page.
  • GitHub Profile Additional repositories and versioned project material.
  • Artwork Selected work, collaborations, works in progress, and additional archives.
  • Contact Public contact page.
